architecture_fit.note: Single hand-built attention head produces positive colour separation, but since the coloring is proper by construction S*adj equals plain adj, so the projector is redundant and it reduces to row-normalised adjacency.

baseline_comparison.note: Beats the task's built-in uniform baseline (lift 0.033 > 0), but adds no own ablation — e.g. zeroing the projector — which would have exposed its redundancy.

faithfulness.note: Purely synthetic hand-built circuit; README acknowledges no training but proposes no causal/ablation check and never notes that the colour computation is a no-op given proper colorings.

operating_range.note: Swept over n in {20,40,60} (~3x, not 2 orders) and p in {0.1,0.2,0.3}; separation decays with n (0.068->0.033->0.020) but README does not analyse the degradation.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Genuinely hand-set circuit: fixed off-diagonal projector P built on cuda, no learned weights, reproduces the target behaviour.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab is only a Markdown dump of six scalar metrics — no chart, heatmap, or per-graph same-vs-diff comparison to make the claim legible.

visualisation_rationale.note: README argues the analytic structure is known so stats suffice; reasoned and tied to the metric table, but self-serving in justifying the absence of any real visualisation.

notes: A clean, minimal hand-built attention head that does beat the uniform baseline and earns the hardcoded-weights bonus. The core weakness is conceptual: because the greedy coloring is proper, masking colour-difference scores by adjacency is identical to plain row-normalised adjacency, so the projector never actually discriminates colour — and no ablation or visualisation surfaces this. Demo is a bare text metric dump rather than a real visualisation. Mean of scored items ~2.86 → borderline.
